channel.title: Journal of the NSSS – National Security Social Society

image.title: Journal of the NSSS – National Security Social Society

item.title: Representative Showcase of a Student Essay
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description: This post is representative of how student essays, solicited for and published as part of the “Journal of the NSSS” (placeholder title), will be presented. Submissions will be requested in a DOCX, ODT, MD or other format amenable to transferring relatively structured text into the WordPress editor. Procedures for streamlining that on the backend are […]
item.content:encoded:
This post is representative of how student essays, solicited for and published as part of the “Journal of the NSSS” (placeholder title), will be presented. Submissions will be requested in a DOCX, ODT, MD or other format amenable to transferring relatively structured text into the WordPress editor. Procedures for streamlining that on the backend are being developed. One aspect of those procedures we’ve already implemented are Markdown-style footnotes using a WordPress plugin.1 These work in the following manner:

This is a codeblock, here being used to represent how you would input this text into the WordPress editor. You won't find much use for these in formatting student essay submissions, probably. To create a markdown footnote you simply write[^1] in a document.

Add whatever other text you like after this, including more footnotes. 

To clarify: there is a dedicated footnote utility within the WordPress backend, but it's surprisingly hard to get automated conversion to that from DOCX without things fouling up. Pandoc + Markdown is consistent as far as we can tell. But we're doing more experiments here, so watch this space.

[^1]: Then at the bottom you add a line like so, including the footnote text. These will render to HTML correctly, and are clickable.

There are wide range of formatting options on offer which can readily handle the types of content you’d expect out of student essays.

Headings, for example.

Yes, headings.

Headings of various sizes, even. (this is an H2)

And we can continue this.

To H3 and beyond.

Mind you, most students won’t use more than one layer of headings.

Another thing we can do is tables:

In the midst of regularly formatted text we can present blockquotes:

Which are displayed like this.

And can themselves contain arbitrary content, including tables, images, headings, etc.

And of course markdown footnotes.2

Another thing that you might find in some student essays. Images. WordPress has on the backend a quite straightforward feature for uploading images, so you can use any that would be provided in the original paper (assuming they aren’t under a restrictive license, of course.)

By way of conclusion, it’s time to conclude.

Conclusion

In conclusion, the current working concept of the format has a block of metadata at the end. Something like this:

AUTHOR: Student Name
COURSE: NSPO80XX National Security College Studies
CONVENER: Dr. Course Convener
YEAR: 2024 Semester 2
MARK: 99 HD

(The reason we don’t put this at the top is because it looks ugly in text previews, and the only real way to avoid that is to turn off text previews for query loop displays like that on the front page. Which we have done. But we may not want to do so in future.)

1. Like this.

2. A blockquote I found somewhere.

item.title: The Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty in the 21st Century: Balancing Global Security, Diplomacy, and the Quest for Nuclear Disarmament
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit1. Sed euismod, magna vel faucibus tempor, quam velit eleifend sapien, at ullamcorper quam nisl eget massa. Nulla facilisi. Donec vitae justo at nunc vulputate iaculis ut non lectus2. In hac habitasse platea dictumst. Aliquam erat volutpat3, integer cursus dapibus justo, nec varius ex blandit id. Vestibulum ante […]
item.content:encoded:
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit1. Sed euismod, magna vel faucibus tempor, quam velit eleifend sapien, at ullamcorper quam nisl eget massa. Nulla facilisi. Donec vitae justo at nunc vulputate iaculis ut non lectus2. In hac habitasse platea dictumst.

Aliquam erat volutpat3, integer cursus dapibus justo, nec varius ex blandit id. Vestibulum ante ipsum primis in faucibus orci luctus et ultrices posuere cubilia curae; Suspendisse potenti. Phasellus ornare, ex a efficitur sagittis4, mi mauris lacinia erat, in ullamcorper ligula sapien at nulla.

Curabitur tincidunt, ex at hendrerit auctor5, mi augue dapibus justo, nec interdum enim justo nec ante. Morbi auctor, lectus vitae bibendum elementum, purus dui porta est, vitae sollicitudin ligula tellus ut elit. Aenean id cursus libero.

AUTHOR: Simon Security
COURSE: NSPO8066 Security in the Nuclear Domain
CONVENER: Dr. Nathan Nuclear
YEAR: 2024 Semester 1
MARK: 90 HD

1. This is a footnote demonstrating the use of markdown for adding additional information or references.
2. Another footnote, explaining that this part of the text might refer to a specific condition or source.
3. Here, we use a markdown footnote to elaborate on the previous sentence’s meaning or to cite a source.
4. Footnotes can be used to provide context for uncommon terms or historical references, as illustrated here.
5. Demonstrating how footnotes can be inserted amidst a paragraph to clarify a term or add depth to the text without interrupting the flow.

item.title: Exploring the Intersection of Cybersecurity and National Security: Strategies for Safeguarding Critical Infrastructure in the Age of Digital Espionage

Donec vitae justo at nunc vulputate iaculis ut non lectus2. In hac habitasse platea dictumst. Aliquam erat volutpat3, integer cursus dapibus justo, nec varius ex blandit id. Vestibulum ante […]
item.content:encoded:
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit1. Sed euismod, magna vel faucibus tempor, quam velit eleifend sapien, at ullamcorper quam nisl eget massa. Nulla facilisi. Donec vitae justo at nunc vulputate iaculis ut non lectus2. In hac habitasse platea dictumst.

Aliquam erat volutpat3, integer cursus dapibus justo, nec varius ex blandit id. Vestibulum ante ipsum primis in faucibus orci luctus et ultrices posuere cubilia curae; Suspendisse potenti. Phasellus ornare, ex a efficitur sagittis4, mi mauris lacinia erat, in ullamcorper ligula sapien at nulla.

Curabitur tincidunt, ex at hendrerit auctor5, mi augue dapibus justo, nec interdum enim justo nec ante. Morbi auctor, lectus vitae bibendum elementum, purus dui porta est, vitae sollicitudin ligula tellus ut elit. Aenean id cursus libero.

AUTHOR: Sharon Security
COURSE: NSPO8066 Security in the Cyber Domain
CONVENER: Dr. Nicolette National
YEAR: 2024 Semester 9
MARK: 99 HD

1. This is a footnote demonstrating the use of markdown for adding additional information or references.
2. Another footnote, explaining that this part of the text might refer to a specific condition or source.
3. Here, we use a markdown footnote to elaborate on the previous sentence’s meaning or to cite a source.
4. Footnotes can be used to provide context for uncommon terms or historical references, as illustrated here.
5. Demonstrating how footnotes can be inserted amidst a paragraph to clarify a term or add depth to the text without interrupting the flow.

item.title: Maritime Security and the Indian Ocean Region: A Case Study on Cooperative Strategies for Combating Piracy and Ensuring Regional Stability
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description: The Indian Ocean Region (IOR), a crucible of global trade routes, handles about 40% of the world’s seaborne oil trade and a third of international maritime commerce,1 making it a strategic focal point. Piracy, a persistent menace, exemplified by the Somali piracy crisis circa 2010, with over 200 attacks,2 underscores the necessity for collective action. […]
item.content:encoded:
The Indian Ocean Region (IOR), a crucible of global trade routes, handles about 40% of the world’s seaborne oil trade and a third of international maritime commerce,1 making it a strategic focal point. Piracy, a persistent menace, exemplified by the Somali piracy crisis circa 2010, with over 200 attacks,2 underscores the necessity for collective action. International efforts such as the Combined Maritime Forces (CMF)3 and Operation Atalanta4 have shown results, as demonstrated by the decline in piracy incidents. These initiatives, complemented by information-sharing platforms like MSCHOA5 and BMP guidelines, illustrate the power of coordinated strategies.

However, the security landscape is shifting, with emerging threats in different parts of the IOR,6 necessitating an adaptive approach. India’s SAGAR policy7 reflects a vision for comprehensive regional security, embracing economic growth and shared responsibility among littoral states. Moving forward, a holistic national security policy in the IOR must integrate environmental, economic, and legal dimensions to tackle the evolving maritime security challenges effectively.

AUTHOR: Samson Security
COURSE: NSPO8098 Security in the Maritime Domain
CONVENER: Dr. Nicolette National
YEAR: 2024 Semester 2
MARK: 88 HD

  1. International Maritime Organization. (2019). Piracy and Armed Robbery against Ships. [Accessed from link]︎

  2. United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime. (2011). Piracy off the Coast of Somalia: The Economic and Social Impact.︎

  3. Combined Maritime Forces. (n.d.). About CMF. [Retrieved from website]︎

  4. European Union Naval Force. (n.d.). Operation Atalanta. [Retrieved from official page]︎

  5. Maritime Security Centre – Horn of Africa. (n.d.). About MSCHOA. [Online resource]︎

  6. International Maritime Bureau. (2020). IMB Piracy Report 2020.︎

  7. Ministry of External Affairs, India. (2015). SAGAR – Security And Growth for All in the Region. [Press release]︎
